title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the
indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input has **exactly one solution**, and you may not
use the same element twice. You can return the answer in any order.

solutions.summary: Check every pair of indices until a matching sum is found.

steps.title: Iterate over the first index
steps.explanation: Loop over each index `i`. For each `i` we will look for a partner that completes the target sum.

steps.title: Scan the rest for a match
steps.explanation: For each `i`, scan every later index `j`. If `nums[i] + nums[j] === target`, return the pair.

solutions.summary: Remember each value as you go; look up the complement in O(1).

steps.title: Track seen values
steps.explanation: Create a hash map from value to index. As we iterate we record each number we have seen so far.

steps.title: Look up the complement first
steps.explanation: Before storing the current number, check whether its complement (`target - n`) is already in the map. If so, we have our answer in a single pass.
